NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Hear this, all peoples; Give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
NKJV.2: Both low and high, Rich and poor together.
NKJV.3: My mouth shall speak wisdom, And the meditation of my heart shall give understanding.
NKJV.4: I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will disclose my dark saying on the harp.
NKJV.5: Why should I fear in the days of evil, When the iniquity at my heels surrounds me?
NKJV.6: Those who trust in their wealth And boast in the multitude of their riches,
NKJV.7: None of them can by any means redeem his brother, Nor give to God a ransom for him—
NKJV.8: For the redemption of their souls is costly, And it shall cease forever—
NKJV.9: That he should continue to live eternally, And not see the Pit.
NKJV.10: For he sees wise men die; Likewise the fool and the senseless person perish, And leave their wealth to others.
NKJV.11: Their inner thought is that their houses will last forever, Their dwelling places to all generations; They call their lands after their own names.
NKJV.12: Nevertheless man, though in honor, does not remain; He is like the beasts that perish.
NKJV.13: This is the way of those who are foolish, And of their posterity who approve their sayings. Selah
NKJV.14: Like sheep they are laid in the grave; Death shall feed on them;  The upright shall have dominion over them in the morning;  And their beauty shall be consumed in the grave, far from their dwelling.
NKJV.15: But God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave, For He shall receive me. Selah
NKJV.16: Do not be afraid when one becomes rich, When the glory of his house is increased;
NKJV.17: For when he dies he shall carry nothing away; His glory shall not descend after him.
NKJV.18: Though while he lives he blesses himself (For men will praise you when you do well for yourself),
NKJV.19: He shall go to the generation of his fathers; They shall never see light.
NKJV.20: A man who is in honor, yet does not understand, Is like the beasts that perish.

KJV.4: I will incline<S>5186</S> mine ear<S>241</S> to a parable<S>4912</S>: I will open<S>6605</S> my dark saying<S>2420</S> upon the harp<S>3658</S>.
KJV.5: Wherefore should I fear<S>3372</S> in the days<S>3117</S> of evil<S>7451</S>, when the iniquity<S>5771</S> of my heels<S>6120</S> shall compass<S>5437</S> me about?

KJV.7: None of them can<S>376</S> by any means<S>6299</S> redeem<S>6299</S> his brother<S>251</S>, nor give<S>5414</S> to God<S>430</S> a ransom<S>3724</S> for him:
KJV.8: (For the redemption<S>6306</S> of their soul<S>5315</S> is precious<S>3365</S>, and it ceaseth<S>2308</S> for ever<S>5769</S>:)
KJV.9: That he should still live<S>2421</S> for ever<S>5331</S>, and not see<S>7200</S> corruption<S>7845</S>.

KJV.11: Their inward<S>7130</S> thought is, that their houses<S>1004</S> shall continue for ever<S>5769</S>, and their dwelling<S>4908</S> places to all<S>1755</S> generations<S>1755</S>; they call<S>7121</S> their lands<S>127</S> after their own names<S>8034</S>. <sup>all: Heb. generation and generation</sup>
KJV.12: Nevertheless man<S>120</S> being in honour<S>3366</S> abideth<S>3885</S> not: he is like<S>4911</S> the beasts<S>929</S> that perish<S>1820</S>.
KJV.13: This their way<S>1870</S> is their folly<S>3689</S>: yet their posterity<S>310</S> approve<S>7521</S> their sayings<S>6310</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>approve: Heb. delight in their mouth</sup>
KJV.14: Like sheep<S>6629</S> they are laid<S>8371</S> in the grave<S>7585</S>; death<S>4194</S> shall feed<S>7462</S> on them; and the upright<S>3477</S> shall have dominion<S>7287</S> over them in the morning<S>1242</S>; and their beauty<S>6697</S> <S>6736</S> shall consume<S>1086</S> in the grave<S>7585</S> from their dwelling<S>2073</S>. <sup>beauty: or, strength</sup> <sup>in the grave from: or, the grave being an habitation to every one of them</sup>
KJV.15: But God<S>430</S> will redeem<S>6299</S> my soul<S>5315</S> from the power<S>3027</S> of the grave<S>7585</S>: for he shall receive<S>3947</S> me. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>power: Heb. hand</sup> <sup>the grave: or, hell</sup>
KJV.16: Be not thou afraid<S>3372</S> when one<S>376</S> is made rich<S>6238</S>, when the glory<S>3519</S> of his house<S>1004</S> is increased<S>7235</S>;

KJV.19: He shall go<S>935</S> to the generation<S>1755</S> of his fathers<S>1</S>; they shall never<S>3808</S> <S>5331</S> see<S>7200</S> light<S>216</S>. <sup>He: Heb. The soul shall</sup>
KJV.20: Man<S>120</S> that is in honour<S>3366</S>, and understandeth<S>995</S> not, is like<S>4911</S> the beasts<S>929</S> that perish<S>1820</S>.

ESV.1: Hear this, all peoples! Give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
ESV.2: both low and high, rich and poor together!
ESV.3: My mouth shall speak wisdom; the meditation of my heart shall be understanding.
ESV.4: I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will solve my riddle to the music of the lyre.
ESV.5: Why should I fear in times of trouble, when the iniquity of those who cheat me surrounds me,
ESV.6: those who trust in their wealth and boast of the abundance of their riches?
ESV.7: Truly no man can ransom another, or give to God the price of his life,
ESV.8: for the ransom of their life is costly and can never suffice,
ESV.9: that he should live on forever and never see the pit.
ESV.10: For he sees that even the wise die; the fool and the stupid alike must perish and leave their wealth to others.
ESV.11: Their graves are their homes forever,  their dwelling places to all generations, though they called lands by their own names.
ESV.12: Man in his pomp will not remain; he is like the beasts that perish.
ESV.13: This is the path of those who have foolish confidence; yet after them people approve of their boasts. Selah
ESV.14: Like sheep they are appointed for Sheol; death shall be their shepherd, and the upright shall rule over them in the morning. Their form shall be consumed in Sheol, with no place to dwell.
ESV.15: But God will ransom my soul from the power of Sheol, for he will receive me. Selah
ESV.16: Be not afraid when a man becomes rich, when the glory of his house increases.
ESV.17: For when he dies he will carry nothing away; his glory will not go down after him.
ESV.18: For though, while he lives, he counts himself blessed — and though you get praise when you do well for yourself —
ESV.19: his soul will go to the generation of his fathers, who will never again see light.
ESV.20: Man in his pomp yet without understanding is like the beasts that perish.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Hear this, all peoples; Give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
NASB.2: Both low and high, Rich and poor together.
NASB.3: My mouth will speak wisdom, And the meditation of my heart [will be] understanding.
NASB.4: I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will express my riddle on the harp.
NASB.5: Why should I fear in days of adversity, When the iniquity of my foes surrounds me,
NASB.6: Even those who trust in their wealth And boast in the abundance of their riches?
NASB.7: No man can by any means redeem [his] brother Or give to God a ransom for him--
NASB.8: For the redemption of his soul is costly, And he should cease [trying] forever--
NASB.9: That he should live on eternally, That he should not undergo decay.
NASB.10: For he sees [that even] wise men die; The stupid and the senseless alike perish And leave their wealth to others.
NASB.11: Their inner thought is [that] their houses are forever [And] their dwelling places to all generations; They have called their lands after their own names.
NASB.12: But man in [his] pomp will not endure; He is like the beasts that perish.
NASB.13: This is the way of those who are foolish, And of those after them who approve their words. Selah.
NASB.14: As sheep they are appointed for Sheol; Death shall be their shepherd; And the upright shall rule over them in the morning, And their form shall be for Sheol to consume So that they have no habitation.
NASB.15: But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol, For He will receive me. Selah.
NASB.16: Do not be afraid when a man becomes rich, When the glory of his house is increased;
NASB.17: For when he dies he will carry nothing away; His glory will not descend after him.
NASB.18: Though while he lives he congratulates himself-- And though [men] praise you when you do well for yourself--
NASB.19: He shall go to the generation of his fathers; They will never see the light.
NASB.20: Man in [his] pomp, yet without understanding, Is like the beasts that perish.

NLT.1:
Listen to this, all you people!
Pay attention, everyone in the world!
NLT.2:
High and low,
rich and poor — listen!
NLT.3:
For my words are wise,
and my thoughts are filled with insight.
NLT.4:
I listen carefully to many proverbs
and solve riddles with inspiration from a harp.
NLT.5:
Why should I fear when trouble comes,
when enemies surround me?
NLT.6:
They trust in their wealth
and boast of great riches.
NLT.7:
Yet they cannot redeem themselves from death
by paying a ransom to God.
NLT.8:
Redemption does not come so easily,
for no one can ever pay enough
NLT.9:
to live forever
and never see the grave.
NLT.10:
Those who are wise must finally die,
just like the foolish and senseless,
leaving all their wealth behind.
NLT.11:
The grave is their eternal home,
where they will stay forever.
They may name their estates after themselves,
NLT.12:
but their fame will not last.
They will die, just like animals.
NLT.13:
This is the fate of fools,
though they are remembered as being wise. Interlude
NLT.14:
Like sheep, they are led to the grave,
where death will be their shepherd.
In the morning the godly will rule over them.
Their bodies will rot in the grave,
far from their grand estates.
NLT.15:
But as for me, God will redeem my life.
He will snatch me from the power of the grave. Interlude
NLT.16:
So don’t be dismayed when the wicked grow rich
and their homes become ever more splendid.
NLT.17:
For when they die, they take nothing with them.
Their wealth will not follow them into the grave.
NLT.18:
In this life they consider themselves fortunate
and are applauded for their success.
NLT.19:
But they will die like all before them
and never again see the light of day.
NLT.20:
People who boast of their wealth don’t understand;
they will die, just like animals.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by the sons of Korah. Hear this, all you peoples. Listen, all you inhabitants of the world,
WEB.2: both low and high, rich and poor together.
WEB.3: My mouth will speak words of wisdom. My heart shall utter understanding.
WEB.4: I will incline my ear to a proverb. I will open my riddle on the harp.
WEB.5: Why should I fear in the days of evil, when iniquity at my heels surrounds me?
WEB.6: Those who trust in their wealth, and boast in the multitude of their riches —
WEB.7: none of them can by any means redeem his brother, nor give God a ransom for him.
WEB.8: For the redemption of their life is costly, no payment is ever enough,
WEB.9: that he should live on forever, that he should not see corruption.
WEB.10: For he sees that wise men die; likewise the fool and the senseless perish, and leave their wealth to others.
WEB.11: Their inward thought is that their houses will endure forever, and their dwelling places to all generations. They name their lands after themselves.
WEB.12: But man, despite his riches, doesn’t endure. He is like the animals that perish.
WEB.13: This is the destiny of those who are foolish, and of those who approve their sayings. Selah.
WEB.14: They are appointed as a flock for Sheol.  Death shall be their shepherd. The upright shall have dominion over them in the morning. Their beauty shall decay in Sheol,  far from their mansion.
WEB.15: But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol, for he will receive me. Selah.
WEB.16: Don’t be afraid when a man is made rich, when the glory of his house is increased;
WEB.17: for when he dies he will carry nothing away. His glory won’t descend after him.
WEB.18: Though while he lived he blessed his soul — and men praise you when you do well for yourself —
WEB.19: he shall go to the generation of his fathers. They shall never see the light.
WEB.20: A man who has riches without understanding, is like the animals that perish.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of the sons of Korah. [1] Hear this, all you peoples;
listen, all who inhabit the world, ⓖ
CSB17.2:
both low and high,
rich and poor together. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
My mouth speaks wisdom;
my heart’s meditation brings understanding. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
I turn my ear to a proverb;
I explain my riddle with a lyre. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
Why should I fear in times of trouble?
The iniquity of my foes surrounds me. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
They trust in their wealth
and boast of their abundant riches. ⓛ
CSB17.7:
Yet these cannot redeem a person [1]
or pay his ransom to God ⓜ  —
CSB17.8:
since the price of redeeming him is too costly,
one should forever stop trying [2] ⓝ  —
CSB17.9:
so that he may live forever
and not see the Pit. ⓐ
CSB17.10:
For one can see that the wise die;
the foolish and stupid also pass away. ⓑ
Then they leave their wealth to others. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
Their graves are their permanent homes, [3]
their dwellings from generation to generation,
though they have named estates after themselves. ⓓ
CSB17.12:
But despite his assets, [4] mankind will not last;
he is like the animals that perish. ⓔ
CSB17.13:
This is the way of those who are arrogant,
and of their followers,
who approve of their words. [5] ⓕ Selah
CSB17.14:
Like sheep they are headed for Sheol;
Death will shepherd them.
The upright will rule over them in the morning, ⓖ
and their form will waste away in Sheol, [6]
far from their lofty abode. ⓗ
CSB17.15:
But God will redeem me
from the power of Sheol,
for he will take me. ⓘ Selah
CSB17.16:
Do not be afraid when a person gets rich,
when the wealth [7] of his house increases. ⓙ
CSB17.17:
For when he dies, he will take nothing at all;
his wealth will not follow him down. ⓚ
CSB17.18:
Though he blesses himself during his lifetime —
and you are acclaimed when you do well for yourself ⓛ  —
CSB17.19:
he will go to the generation of his fathers;
they will never see the light. ⓜ
CSB17.20:
Mankind, with his assets
but without understanding,
is like the animals that perish. ⓝ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. Hear this, all peoples;
Listen carefully, all inhabitants of the world,
AMP.2:
Both low and high,
Rich and poor together:
AMP.3:
My mouth will speak wisdom,
And the meditation of my heart will be understanding.
AMP.4:
I will incline my ear and consent to a proverb;
On the lyre I will unfold my riddle.
AMP.5:
Why should I fear in the days of evil,
When the wickedness of those who would betray me surrounds me [on every side],
AMP.6:
Even those who trust in and rely on their wealth
And boast of the abundance of their riches?
AMP.7:
None of them can by any means redeem [either himself or] his brother,
Nor give to God a ransom for him—
AMP.8:
For the ransom of his soul is too costly,
And he should cease trying forever—
AMP.9:
So that he should live on eternally,
That he should never see the pit (grave) and undergo decay.
AMP.10:
For he sees that even wise men die;
The fool and the stupid alike perish
And leave their wealth to others.
AMP.11:
Their inward thought is that their houses will continue forever,
And their dwelling places to all generations;
They have named their lands after their own names [ignoring God].
AMP.12:
But man, with all his [self] honor and pomp, will not endure;
He is like the beasts that perish.
AMP.13:
This is the fate of those who are foolishly confident,
And of those after them who approve [and are influenced by] their words. Selah.
AMP.14:
Like sheep they are appointed for Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead);
Death will be their shepherd;
And the upright shall rule over them in the morning,
And their form and beauty shall be for Sheol to consume,
So that they have no dwelling [on earth].
AMP.15:
But God will redeem my life from the power of Sheol,
For He will receive me. Selah.
AMP.16:
Be not afraid when [an ungodly] man becomes rich,
When the wealth and glory of his house are increased;
AMP.17:
For when he dies he will carry nothing away;
His glory will not descend after him.
AMP.18:
Though while he lives he counts himself happy and prosperous—
And though people praise you when you do well for yourself—
AMP.19:
He shall go to the generation of his fathers;
They shall never again see the light.
AMP.20:
A man [who is held] in honor,
Yet who lacks [spiritual] understanding and a teachable heart, is like the beasts that perish.

MSG.1:
A psalm of the sons of Korah
Listen, everyone, listen—
earth-dwellers, don't miss this.
MSG.2:
All you haves
and have-nots,
All together now: listen.
MSG.3:
I set plainspoken wisdom before you,
my heart-seasoned understandings of life.
MSG.4:
I fine-tuned my ear to the sayings of the wise,
I solve life's riddle with the help of a harp.
MSG.5:
So why should I fear in bad times,
hemmed in by enemy malice,
MSG.6:
Shoved around by bullies,
demeaned by the arrogant rich?
MSG.7:
Really! There's no such thing as self-rescue,
pulling yourself up by your bootstraps.
MSG.8:
The cost of rescue is beyond our means,
and even then it doesn't guarantee
MSG.9:
Life forever, or insurance
against the Black Hole.
MSG.10:
Anyone can see that the brightest and best die,
wiped out right along with fools and dunces.
MSG.11:
They leave all their prowess behind,
move into their new home, The Coffin,
The cemetery their permanent address.
And to think they named counties after themselves!
MSG.12:
We aren't immortal. We don't last long.
Like our dogs, we age and weaken. And die.
MSG.13:
This is what happens to those who live for the moment,
who only look out for themselves:
MSG.14:
Death herds them like sheep straight to hell;
they disappear down the gullet of the grave;
They waste away to nothing—
nothing left but a marker in a cemetery.
MSG.15:
But me? God snatches me from the clutch of death,
he reaches down and grabs me.
MSG.16:
So don't be impressed with those who get rich
and pile up fame and fortune.
MSG.17:
They can't take it with them;
fame and fortune all get left behind.
MSG.18:
Just when they think they've arrived
and folks praise them because they've made good,
MSG.19:
They enter the family burial plot
where they'll never see sunshine again.
MSG.20:
We aren't immortal. We don't last long.
Like our dogs, we age and weaken. And die.

NIV.1:
Psalm 49
For the director of music. Of the Sons of Korah. A psalm.
Hear this, all you peoples;
listen, all who live in this world,
NIV.2:
both low and high,
rich and poor alike:
NIV.3:
My mouth will speak words of wisdom;
the utterance from my heart will give understanding.
NIV.4:
I will turn my ear to a proverb;
with the harp I will expound my riddle:
NIV.5:
Why should I fear when evil days come,
when wicked deceivers surround me—
NIV.6:
those who trust in their wealth
and boast of their great riches?
NIV.7:
No man can redeem the life of another
or give to God a ransom for him—
NIV.8:
the ransom for a life is costly,
no payment is ever enough—
NIV.9:
that he should live on forever
and not see decay.
NIV.10:
For all can see that wise men die;
the foolish and the senseless alike perish
and leave their wealth to others.
NIV.11:
Their tombs will remain their houses forever,
their dwellings for endless generations,
though they had named lands after themselves.
NIV.12:
But man, despite his riches, does not endure;
he is like the beasts that perish.
NIV.13:
This is the fate of those who trust in themselves,
and of their followers, who approve their sayings. Selah
NIV.14:
Like sheep they are destined for the grave,
and death will feed on them.
The upright will rule over them in the morning;
their forms will decay in the grave,
far from their princely mansions.
NIV.15:
But God will redeem my life from the grave;
he will surely take me to himself. Selah
NIV.16:
Do not be overawed when a man grows rich,
when the splendor of his house increases;
NIV.17:
for he will take nothing with him when he dies,
his splendor will not descend with him.
NIV.18:
Though while he lived he counted himself blessed—
and men praise you when you prosper—
NIV.19:
he will join the generation of his fathers,
who will never see the light of life.
NIV.20:
A man who has riches without understanding
is like the beasts that perish.

NRSVCE.1:
Hear this, all you peoples;
give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
NRSVCE.2:
both low and high,
rich and poor together.
NRSVCE.3:
My mouth shall speak wisdom;
the meditation of my heart shall be understanding.
NRSVCE.4:
I will incline my ear to a proverb;
I will solve my riddle to the music of the harp.
NRSVCE.5:
Why should I fear in times of trouble,
when the iniquity of my persecutors surrounds me,
NRSVCE.6:
those who trust in their wealth
and boast of the abundance of their riches?
NRSVCE.7:
Truly, no ransom avails for one’s life,
there is no price one can give to God for it.
NRSVCE.8:
For the ransom of life is costly,
and can never suffice,
NRSVCE.9:
that one should live on forever
and never see the grave.
NRSVCE.10:
When we look at the wise, they die;
fool and dolt perish together
and leave their wealth to others.
NRSVCE.11:
Their graves are their homes forever,
their dwelling places to all generations,
though they named lands their own.
NRSVCE.12:
Mortals cannot abide in their pomp;
they are like the animals that perish.
NRSVCE.13:
Such is the fate of the foolhardy,
the end of those who are pleased with their lot. Selah
NRSVCE.14:
Like sheep they are appointed for Sheol;
Death shall be their shepherd;
straight to the grave they descend,
and their form shall waste away;
Sheol shall be their home.
NRSVCE.15:
But God will ransom my soul from the power of Sheol,
for he will receive me. Selah
NRSVCE.16:
Do not be afraid when some become rich,
when the wealth of their houses increases.
NRSVCE.17:
For when they die they will carry nothing away;
their wealth will not go down after them.
NRSVCE.18:
Though in their lifetime they count themselves happy
— for you are praised when you do well for yourself —
NRSVCE.19:
they will go to the company of their ancestors,
who will never again see the light.
NRSVCE.20:
Mortals cannot abide in their pomp;
they are like the animals that perish.

NET.1: Listen to this, all you nations! Pay attention, all you inhabitants of the world!
NET.2: Pay attention, all you people, both rich and poor!
NET.3: I will declare a wise saying; I will share my profound thoughts.
NET.4: I will learn a song that imparts wisdom; I will then sing my insightful song to the accompaniment of a harp.
NET.5: Why should I be afraid in times of trouble, when the sinful deeds of deceptive men threaten to overwhelm me?
NET.6: They trust in their wealth and boast in their great riches.
NET.7: Certainly a man cannot rescue his brother; he cannot pay God an adequate ransom price
NET.8: (the ransom price for a human life is too high, and people go to their final destiny),
NET.9: so that he might continue to live forever and not experience death.
NET.10: Surely one sees that even wise people die; fools and spiritually insensitive people all pass away and leave their wealth to others.
NET.11: Their grave becomes their permanent residence, their eternal dwelling place. They name their lands after themselves,
NET.12: but, despite their wealth, people do not last, they are like animals that perish.
NET.13: This is the destiny of fools, and of those who approve of their philosophy. (Selah)
NET.14: They will travel to Sheol like sheep, with death as their shepherd. The godly will rule over them when the day of vindication dawns; Sheol will consume their bodies and they will no longer live in impressive houses.
NET.15: But God will rescue my life from the power of Sheol; certainly he will pull me to safety. (Selah)
NET.16: Do not be afraid when a man becomes rich and his wealth multiplies!
NET.17: For he will take nothing with him when he dies; his wealth will not follow him down into the grave.
NET.18: He pronounces this blessing on himself while he is alive: “May men praise you, for you have done well!”
NET.19: But he will join his ancestors; they will never again see the light of day.
NET.20: Wealthy people do not understand; they are like animals that perish.
